title: Aloo Chop

ingredients.title: For the Potato Filling
items: 500 g potatoes (starchy variety like russet or gold), boiled, peeled, and mashed
1 ½ tbsp mustard oil (or vegetable oil)
1 tsp panch phoron (Bengali five spice mix: fenugreek, nigella, cumin, black mustard, fennel seeds)
1–2 green chilies, finely chopped (adjust to taste)
1 tbsp fresh coriander leaves, finely chopped
Juice of ½ lime (optional, for brightness)

ingredients.title: For the Batter
items: ¼ tsp baking soda (for extra crispness)
About 180–200 ml water (adjust for a smooth, coating batter)

ingredients.title: For Frying
items: 500 ml mustard oil or vegetable oil (for deep frying)

instruction.title: Step 1 – Prepare the Filling
steps: Heat mustard oil in a heavy pan until slightly smoking (to remove raw aroma), then lower the heat.
Add panch phoron; let the seeds crackle and release their aroma.
Add onions, sauté until soft and lightly golden.
Stir in green chilies and grated ginger; cook for 30 seconds.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, and garam masala; sauté for 20–30 seconds.
Add mashed potatoes, salt, and mix thoroughly so spices are evenly distributed.
Stir in fresh coriander leaves and lime juice. Adjust seasoning.
Let the mixture cool, then divide into 12–15 equal portions and shape into flat discs or ovals.

instruction.title: Step 2 – Make the Batter
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine besan, turmeric, red chili powder, baking soda, and salt.
Add water gradually, whisking until you have a lump-free batter of medium-thick consistency (should coat the back of a spoon without dripping too fast).

instruction.title: Step 3 – Fry the Aloo Chops
steps: Heat oil in a deep wok or kadhai to 170–175 °C (medium-high heat).
Dip each potato disc into the batter, ensuring a complete coat, and gently lower into the hot oil.
Fry in batches without overcrowding, turning occasionally, until crisp and golden (about 3–4 minutes per batch).
Drain on a wire rack or paper towels to remove excess oil.

instruction.title: Step 4 – Serve
steps: Serve hot with kasundi (Bengali mustard sauce) or green chutney, and onion salad.
Best enjoyed fresh; the coating will lose crispness over time.

chefTips: For a smokier, street-style flavor, add a pinch of black salt to the filling.
Using panch phoron makes this distinctly Bengali, but you can use cumin seeds for a simpler version.
Control oil temperature — too low and the chops absorb oil; too high and the exterior burns before the inside heats.
